items.md: ## 62 (6/2) - November 1945 - 52 pages
items.value: 62 (6/2) - November 1945 - 52 pages

items.md: DONALD DUCK - 10 - Donald enters water-ski races and has to operate his tow boat by remote control when the nephews eat themselves sick on popcorn and candy. *(June 27, 1945)*
items.value: DONALD DUCK - 10 - Donald enters water-ski races and has to operate his tow boat by remote control when the nephews eat themselves sick on popcorn and candy. (June 27, 1945)

items.md: **Cross-references:** At the beginning of this story, Donald refers clearly to the story in the preceding issue, Walt Disney's Comics No. 61, October 1945.

items.md: ## 63 (6/3) - December 1945 - 52 pages
items.value: 63 (6/3) - December 1945 - 52 pages

items.md: DONALD DUCK - 10 - Donald advertises to find the rightful owner of a ten-dollar bill the nephews have found, and he is besieged by phony claimants. *(Aug. 2, 1945)*

items.md:
DONALD DUCK - 10 - The nephews obtain a parrot at the docks — "a guy named Joe from Singapore" — and then try to persuade Donald to let them keep it. *(Oct. 4, 1945)*
**Reprinted:** Walt Disney's Comics No. 420, September 1975.

items.md: DONALD DUCK - 10 - The nephews buy miniature jet engines with their savings, against Donald's wishes, but the engines save Donald's life when he and a girl are trapped on a peak. *(Nov. 23, 1945)*
